description: The Hai San Secret Society was a Chinese secret society based in Penang, established around 1820 with origins in Southern China. It was involved in the Larut Wars of 1862–1873, fighting against the Ghee Hin Society for control of tin mines. The society was predominantly Hakka and engaged in bribery, conspiracy, extortion, and mercenary activities. It was dissolved around 1890.

country_links.evidence_quote: was a Penang -based Chinese secret society established around 1820... At Larut , miners who were members of the Hai San society fought with miners who were members of the Ghee Hin society... The two warring factions also clashed in Selangor .
country_links.context: The Hai San society was based in Penang (now part of Malaysia) and fought in the Larut Wars in Larut and Selangor.

country_links.evidence_quote: Even in the present-day, the ends of justice are frequently defeated both at Pinang, Malacca, and Singapore: by bribery, false swearing, and sometimes by open violence
country_links.context: The Hai San society operated in Singapore, where it defeated justice through bribery and violence.
